memberdef.name: BD_VLAN_TPID_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 3 /* AXI4-Stream status Word offset from * the start of app user words in BD. * TPID is at status word 3, bits * 31-16 */

memberdef.name: BD_VLAN_VID_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 4 /* AXI4-Stream status word offset from * the start of app user words in BD. * VID is at status word 4, bits * 31-16 */

memberdef.name: TPID_MASK
memberdef.initializer: 0xFFFF0000 /* Mask to extract TPID from * the AXI4 STream status * word */

briefdescription.para: This is the Error handler callback function and this function increments the the error counter so that the main thread knows the number of errors.

parameterdescription.para: is a reference to the AxiEthernet device instance.

briefdescription.para: This is the Receive handler function for examples 1 and 2.

detaileddescription.para: It will increment a shared counter, receive and validate the frame.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DMA ring instance.

briefdescription.para: This is the DMA TX Interrupt handler function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to TX channel of the DMA engine.

simplesect.para: This Interrupt handler MUST clear pending interrupts before handling them by calling the call back. Otherwise the following corner case could raise some issue: A packet got transmitted and a TX interrupt got asserted. If the interrupt handler calls the callback before clearing the interrupt, a new packet may get transmitted in the callback. This new packet then can assert one more TX interrupt before the control comes out of the callback function. Now when eventually control comes out of the callback function, it will never know about the second new interrupt and hence while clearing the interrupts, would clear the new interrupt as well and will never process it. To avoid such cases, interrupts must be cleared before calling the callback.

briefdescription.para: This function demonstrates the usage usage of the Axi Ethernet by sending and receiving frames in interrupt driven SGDMA mode.

detaileddescription.para:
AxiEthernetInstancePtr
is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
DmaInstancePtr
is a pointer to the instance of the AXIDMA component.
AxiEthernetBaseAddress
is the base address of the AxiEthernet device.
-XST_SUCCESS to indicate success. -XST_FAILURE to indicate failure.
AxiDma hardware must be initialized before initializing AxiEthernet. Since AxiDma reset line is connected to the AxiEthernet reset line, a reset of AxiDma hardware during its initialization would reset AxiEthernet. In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This example sends and receives a single packet in loopback mode with extended VLAN support.

detaileddescription.para: The transmit frame will have VLAN field populated.
On receive, HW should pass the VLAN field to receive BDs.

AxiEthernetInstancePtr
is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
DmaInstancePtr
is a pointer to the instance of the Dma component.
-XST_SUCCESS to indicate success. -XST_FAILURE to indicate failure.
Summary of VLAN tags handling in this example Frame setup with Tpid1+Cfi1+TxPid => 0x88A83111 Frame translated to TxTransVid => 0x88A83222 Frame tagged to Tpid2+Cfi2+TxTagVid => 0x9100C333 + 0x88A83222 Frame sent and loopbacked.
Frame stripped with RxStrpVid(0x333) => 0x88A83222 Frame translated (key:RxVid:0x222) RxTransVid => 0x88A83444

briefdescription.para: This is the main function for the Axi Ethernet example.

detaileddescription.para: This function is not included if the example is generated from the TestAppGen test tool.

briefdescription.para: This is the DMA TX callback function to be called by TX interrupt handler.

detaileddescription.para: This function handles BDs finished by hardware.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to TX channel of the DMA engine.

briefdescription.para: This is the DMA RX callback function to be called by RX interrupt handler.

detaileddescription.para: This function handles finished BDs by hardware, attaches new buffers to those BDs, and give them back to hardware to receive more incoming packets

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to RX channel of the DMA engine.

briefdescription.para: Implements examples that utilize the Axi Ethernet's interrupt driven SGDMA packet transfer mode to send and receive frames.

listitem.para: How to setup VLAN strip/translate/tag in TX and RX direction
How the VLAN tags are handled
What is the expected VLAN tag(s) after VLAN functions are invoked
AxiEthernetSgDmaIntrExtVlanExample demonstrates the extended VLAN capability. The HW must be setup for extended VLAN for this example to execute.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a asa 4/30/10 First release based on the ll temac driver 1.01a asa 12/10/10 Changes made to enable the AXIDMA Tx/Rx ring interrupts before allocation of Tx/Rx BDs. 3.00a asa 6/25/12 Modified XAxiDma_BdSetLength API call to support new AXI DMA driver version 7.00a. 3.00a bss 10/22/12 Added support for Fast Interrupt Handlers. 3.01a srt 02/14/13 Added support for Zynq (CR 681136) 5.4 ms 01/23/17 Modified xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028. ms 04/05/17 Added tabspace for return statements in functions for proper documentation while generating doxygen. 5.7 rsp 19/12/17 Defined Padding and ExternalLoopback variables.
